steps.title: Spot the Limiting Factor
steps.content: The passage states that “archival specimens are sometimes stored in ways that compromise DNA quality.” The word “sometimes” is critical—it reveals that storage conditions are inconsistent across specimens.

steps.title: Apply the Factor to the Choices
steps.content:
C is correct because it directly reflects this inconsistency: the specimens “may contain valuable genomic data” (when stored well) but “not all of them can yield usable hDNA” (when storage compromises DNA).

The remaining choices either minimize the specimens' worth entirely, add unsupported claims about extraction difficulty, or contradict the passage's statement that the approach remains “relatively underutilized”.

That confirms C is correct.
